stories.headline: A.J. Brown saddles up and rides east to New England
stories.read: Well pardner, the Eagles went and shipped their star wideout to the Patriots for a 2028 first-rounder, and that wagon train shakes up two whole territories. Brown lands next to Drake Maye, a young gunslinger who led the whole league in completion rate and yards per chuck last season, so the big fella is still a No. 1 critter worth huntin'. But here's the vein nobody's struck yet: DeVonta Smith just inherited a fatter cut of the throws in Philly, while Romeo Doubs in New England turned to fool's gold overnight. Pan that Philly creek before your leaguemates wake up.

stories.headline: Nabers' busted wing is mendin' -- 70 to 80 percent down the trail
stories.read: Giants boss John Harbaugh says he's 'very hopeful' Malik Nabers is back soon, reckonin' the young star is 70-to-80 percent through rehab on that knee -- the one that needed a second cuttin' to clear scar tissue. The front office expects him ridin' for the September 13 opener. If the herd gets skittish and lets his price slide come the great land grab, that's cheap gold sittin' in the riverbed -- just keep a spare horse saddled in case the trail turns rough.

stories.headline: Myles Garrett rides west: Browns and Rams swap horses in a blockbuster
stories.read: Cleveland finally let go of the meanest pass-rusher on the frontier, sendin' two-time Defensive Player of the Year Myles Garrett to Los Angeles for young gun Jared Verse and three draft claims. For your outfits that start a defense, the Rams just became the unit to stake early -- pairin' Garrett with that front is a gusher waitin' to blow. Cleveland's defense? That well runs a whole lot drier till Verse settles in, so don't pay yesterday's price for it.
